name: Malaysia Airlines MH17
description: Malaysia Airlines Flight MH17 was a scheduled passenger flight from Amsterdam to Kuala Lumpur that was shot down on 17 July 2014 while flying over eastern Ukraine during the Russo-Ukrainian War. All 283 passengers and 15 crew members were killed. The official investigation, led by the Dutch Safety Board (DSB) and the Joint Investigation Team (JIT), concluded that the aircraft was shot down by a Buk surface-to-air missile launched from territory controlled by pro-Russian separatists. However, various conspiracy theories have emerged surrounding the event, often disputing the official findings and assigning blame to different actors. These theories range from claims that the Ukrainian government was responsible, either intentionally or unintentionally, to accusations against the CIA or other Western intelligence agencies. Proponents of these alternative narratives often cite inconsistencies in the official reports, alleged witness testimonies, and supposed anomalies in radar data or wreckage analysis. They also point to the complex geopolitical context of the conflict in Ukraine and suggest that the downing of MH17 was a deliberate act of provocation or a false flag operation. Mainstream perspectives strongly refute these conspiracy theories, citing overwhelming evidence presented by the JIT, including recovered missile fragments, intercepted communications, and radar data. These perspectives emphasize the rigor and impartiality of the official investigation and dismiss alternative explanations as politically motivated disinformation campaigns aimed at undermining international consensus and obscuring the truth about the incident.
subtitle: Shot down over Ukraine, July 2014.

claims.evidence: Alleged witness accounts, inconsistencies in radar data, supposed motive to provoke Russia
claims.criticism: Overwhelming evidence points to a Buk missile system operated by pro-Russian separatists; witness accounts are unreliable and often contradict each other; radar data has been thoroughly analyzed and does not support this claim.

claims.claim: The CIA orchestrated the downing of MH17 as a false flag operation
claims.evidence: Lack of direct evidence, reliance on circumstantial arguments, geopolitical speculation
claims.criticism: This claim lacks any credible evidence and relies on conjecture and unsubstantiated allegations; the JIT investigation provides a comprehensive and transparent account of the events.

claims.claim: The wreckage shows evidence of machine gun fire from Ukrainian fighter jets
claims.evidence: Images of the wreckage with supposed bullet holes, claims from Russian state media
claims.criticism: The Dutch Safety Board concluded that the damage to the wreckage was consistent with a Buk missile strike and not machine gun fire; the images have been debunked as showing pre-existing damage or damage caused by the missile explosion.

keyFigures.name: Bellingcat
keyFigures.role: Independent investigative journalism group that has debunked many conspiracy theories

timeline.event: Malaysia Airlines Flight MH17 is shot down over eastern Ukraine.
The Dutch Safety Board releases its final report, concluding that MH17 was shot down by a Buk missile.
The Joint Investigation Team concludes that the missile was launched from territory controlled by pro-Russian separatists.
The JIT announces charges against four suspects in connection with the downing of MH17.
